cot_content:
Okay, let's see. The user is asking how to separate egg yolk from the white. The options are A and B. 

Option A says to pass the egg back and forth between hands with spread fingers. Option B says to do it with closed fingers.

Hmm, I remember that when separating eggs, you typically crack the egg and let the yolk fall into one half of the shell, then use your fingers to gently move the yolk back and forth between your hands. The idea is that the white (which is more liquid) will drip off, while the yolk stays intact. 

But wait, if you have spread fingers (option A), you might not have the right control to handle the yolk. If you have your fingers closed, maybe you can create a sort of cup to hold the yolk as you move it. Let me think... when you crack an egg, you usually use your fingers to hold the yolk and pass it between your hands to let the white drip off. So with closed fingers, you can form a sort of cradle for the yolk. If your fingers are spread, the yolk might fall through or be harder to control.

So the correct method is probably option B, passing the egg between hands with closed fingers. That way you can hold the yolk securely while letting the white drip away. 

Wait, but the question is a bit ambiguous. It says "pass egg back and forth between hands" but that seems odd. Normally you crack the egg and pass the yolk, not the whole egg. Maybe the question is referring to a method where you use your fingers to move the yolk after cracking the egg. In that case, you'd use closed fingers to form a sort of cradle. So the answer must be B.
label: minority
